message:
fix(output): retire dead rich suggestions panel

Remove the unused rich-output-only suggestions panel from hook denial rendering and keep pattern suggestions flowing through DenialBox alternatives.

Add focused tests for pattern suggestion formatting, fallback, disable, and display limits.

Closes git_safety_guard-xfpm.

files.patch:
@@ -747,7 +747,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","title":"[CATEGORY] dns.* - DNS Management Pack Suite","description":"# DNS Management Pack Suite

## Overview
DNS is critical infrastructure. Incorrect DNS changes can make services unreachable. Zone deletion or record manipulation affects all users globally.

## Packs to Implement

### dns.cloudflare
- **Why Critical**: Cloudflare manages DNS for millions of domains. Zone/record deletion causes immediate outages.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `cloudflare dns delete` (wrangler)
  - `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../dns_records/`
  - `terraform destroy` on cloudflare resources
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `show`, GET requests

### dns.route53
- **Why Critical**: AWS Route53 handles DNS for AWS-hosted services. Hosted zone deletion affects all records.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws route53 delete-hosted-zone`
  - `aws route53 change-resource-record-sets` with DELETE action
  - `aws route53 delete-health-check`
- **Safe Patterns**: `list-`, `get-`, `test-dns-answer`

### dns.generic
- **Why Critical**: Generic DNS tools (dig, host, nslookup, nsupdate) - nsupdate can modify records.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `nsupdate` with delete commands
  - Zone transfers to untrusted servers
- **Safe Patterns**: `dig`, `host`, `nslookup` (read-only)

## Rationale
DNS changes propagate globally with TTL delays. A bad change can take hours to fix even after correction. Zone delegation changes can effectively transfer domain control.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major DNS providers covered
- [ ] API-based changes detected
- [ ] Record type changes handled appropriately
- [ ] Tests verify propagation-sensitive operations  {"id":"git_safety_guard-x8wj","title":"[PACK] cloud.azure: ACR destructive patterns","description":"# Azure container registry gaps\n\n## Scope\nAdd destructive patterns to cloud.azure pack for ACR repository and image deletion.\n\n## Destructive patterns (examples)\n- az acr repository delete\n- az acr repository untag\n- az acr delete (for registry/resource)\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- [ ] Patterns added with reasons + tests\n- [ ] Keywords updated if needed (acr, registry)\n- [ ] E2E pack cases added","notes":"Updates in src/packs/cloud/azure.rs: added keywords (acr/registry) and destructive patterns for az acr delete, az acr repository delete, az acr repository untag; added unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:00:02.636401346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:39:58.346760186Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:39:58.346760186Z","close_reason":"Patterns + tests added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-x93","title":"[PACK] payment.square - Square Pack","description":"# Square Payment Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Square payment operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### CLI/API Operations
- `square catalog delete`
- `DELETE /v2/catalog/object/{id}`
- Location deletion
- Customer deletion
- Webhook subscription deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `square catalog list`
- `square customers list`
- GET API requests
- Search operations

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: payment.* (git_safety_guard-atw)
- Keywords: square
- Note: Square API uses object-based deletion

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for catalog deletion patterns
- [ ] Unit tests for API DELETE patterns
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/payment_square.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Catalog deletion blocked
- [ ] Customer deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:10:52.679354317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:38:18.677102066Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:38:18.677102066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:10.264073223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.411691888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.496078924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.370170004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.328985986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.452848776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:35.536899759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x93","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:10.218734450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-xkhw","title":"False Positive Bug: DCG blocks non-executable text in heredocs/string literals","description":"## Problem

DCG incorrectly blocks commands where dangerous patterns appear within non-executable contexts like:
- String literals passed as arguments to other commands
- Heredoc content being passed to commands like bd create
- Documentation text containing example commands
- Comments or help text

## Reproduction Case

When attempting to run:
```
bd create --body="Example: rm with dangerous flags on sensitive paths"
```

DCG blocked this because the body TEXT contained patterns matching destructive commands, even though:
1. The text is a string argument to bd create, not an executable command
2. The dangerous-looking text would never be executed
3. Context clearly indicates this is data, not code

## Expected Behavior

DCG should recognize that text within string literals and heredocs passed as DATA to other commands is not executable and should not trigger destructive pattern matching.

## Root Cause Investigation Needed

1. Trace through evaluate_command to see how context classification handles this
2. Check if sanitize_for_pattern_matching properly identifies string contexts
3. Verify span classification distinguishes executable vs data spans
4. Understand why the heredoc/string content is being pattern-matched

## Impact

- Prevents documenting DCG's own behavior with examples
- Blocks legitimate use of dangerous strings in non-executable contexts
- Creates friction when writing tests, docs, or configs containing examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T05:14:39.718578607Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T05:38:56.197016058Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T05:38:56.197016058Z","close_reason":"Fixed: heredocs to non-executing commands (cat, tee, grep, etc.) are now correctly recognized as DATA and allowed. Implemented mask_non_executing_heredocs() and target command detection.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-xld","title":"Epic: CI/CD Maturity & Observability Enhancements","description":"# Epic: CI/CD Maturity & Observability Enhancements

## Background & Context

This epic addresses gaps identified in a comprehensive comparison between DCG's CI/CD setup and CASS (coding_agent_session_search). While DCG already has several advanced CI features (fuzz testing, enforced coverage thresholds, release automation, scheduled deep testing, performance budgets), there are four key areas where CASS has capabilities that would benefit DCG.

## Strategic Goals

DCG is a security-critical tool that guards against destructive commands in AI coding workflows. As such, it requires:

1. **Maximum reliability** - Any bug could either block legitimate work (false positive) or allow destructive commands (false negative)
2. **Performance stability** - Every Bash command passes through DCG, so performance regressions directly impact user experience
3. **Security hardening** - As a security tool, DCG must maintain the highest code quality standards
4. **Maintainability** - Dependencies must stay current to avoid security vulnerabilities

## Gap Analysis (DCG vs CASS)

| Feature | DCG Status | CASS Status | Priority |
|---------|------------|-------------|----------|
| Codecov cloud integration | ❌ Missing | ✅ Has | P2 |
| Memory leak tests | ❌ Missing | ✅ Has | P2 |
| UBS static analysis | ❌ Missing | ✅ Has | P3 |
| Dependabot config | ❌ Missing | ❌ Missing | P3 |

## Why These Matter for DCG

### 1. Codecov Cloud Integration
- **Current state:** Coverage artifacts stored locally, no trend tracking
- **Problem:** Can't see coverage trends over time, no PR coverage diff comments
- **Solution:** Upload to Codecov for historical tracking and PR integration
- **DCG-specific benefit:** Critical paths (evaluator.rs, hook.rs) need visibility

### 2. Memory Leak Detection
- **Current state:** No memory testing
- **Problem:** DCG runs as a hook on EVERY command - memory leaks accumulate
- **Solution:** Add dedicated memory tests with leak detection
- **DCG-specific benefit:** Long Claude Code sessions = thousands of hook invocations

### 3. UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner) Integration
- **Current state:** Only clippy for static analysis
- **Problem:** clippy misses some bug categories UBS catches
- **Solution:** Run UBS on changed files in CI
- **DCG-specific benefit:** Security tool needs defense in depth

### 4. Dependabot Configuration
- **Current state:** Manual dependency updates
- **Problem:** Stale dependencies = security vulnerabilities
- **Solution:** Automated PRs for Cargo and GitHub Actions updates
- **DCG-specific benefit:** fancy-regex, serde_json, etc. need security patches

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Coverage trends visible in Codecov dashboard
- [ ] PR comments show coverage diff
- [ ] Memory tests run in CI, detect leaks
- [ ] UBS runs on PRs, blocks on critical findings
- [ ] Dependabot creates PRs for outdated dependencies
- [ ] All new CI jobs documented in AGENTS.md

## Dependencies

This epic has no blockers - all work can proceed independently.

## Estimated Scope

4 features, ~12-15 subtasks total. Each feature is independently valuable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:19:36.659149026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:39:38.991884354Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:39:38.991884354Z","close_reason":"All 6 child features completed: Codecov (6ao), AGENTS.md docs (96c), E2E extension (cuo), memory tests (woe), Dependabot (7ey), UBS (dis)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ao","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:24:59.474353139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7ey","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:24:59.562194355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-96c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:10:59.473383918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-cuo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:11:00.454454860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-dis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:24:59.533342457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xld","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:24:59.502864705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-xpmx","title":"[E1-T7] Document test subcommand in README","description":"## Task: Document test subcommand in README

### Parent Epic
Epic 1: Pattern Testing Tool (git_safety_guard-nimk)

### What To Do
Add documentation for the test subcommand to README.md with examples and use cases.

### Documentation Sections
1. Basic usage examples
2. All flags and options
3. Output format explanation
4. CI/CD integration examples
5. Troubleshooting tips

### Example Documentation
```markdown
## Testing Commands

Validate your dcg configuration by testing commands without running them:

### Basic Usage
dcg test "rm -rf ./build"

### JSON Output (for CI)
dcg test --json "kubectl delete namespace prod" | jq .outcome

### Using Custom Config
dcg test --config .dcg.production.toml "DROP DATABASE users"
```

### Files to Modify
- README.md

### Dependencies
- E1-T1 through E1-T5 (feature must be complete)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:05:35.345181497Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:20:53.855904028Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:20:53.855885513Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xpmx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6g73","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:34.261433790Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xpmx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6t3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:34.198980210Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-xpmx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-nimk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:39:04.100208031Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
